rationale: In the citing paper, Pocheptsova et al. (2009) is used in hypothesis-building, not just background listing: the authors cite it as core evidence that depleted executive control reduces effortful trade-off comparisons and weakens compromise choices, including a direct quote. This prior logic is explicitly carried into H2a/H2b and then revisited in the discussion to interpret the new findings. Because the citing study advances the same line of argument with a different depletion mechanism and experimental setup, the citation is substantive.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Pocheptsova et al. (2009)'s resource-depletion account of context-dependent choice, especially the reduced compromise effect under depleted cognitive control. It extends that work by testing a neurobiological depletion route (serotonin reduction via ATD) in binding consumer-choice experiments and showing a consistent directional pattern.
